notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: MOLECULAR, CELL, AND DEVELOPMENTAL BIOLOGY B.S.

Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the " Required Major Preparation Courses " section below, each with a grade of C (2.0) or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall. NOTE: BIOL 20L is not required for students who complete the articulated equivalents of BIOL 20A and BIOE 20B at California community colleges. Refer to "Required Major Preparation Courses," Group #2, Section A below for courses approved to satisfy these requirements. Winter Applicants Screening Policy Students applying for winter quarter admission are additionally required to complete the articulated equivalent of CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry . Refer to the " Recommended Preparation " section below for courses approved to articulate to this requirement. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required screening courses, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the " Recommended Preparation " section below, especially CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry and CHEM 8L – Organic Chemistry Laboratory . These courses serve as prerequisites for upper-division requirements, and students who do not complete them prior to transfer will likely need additional quarters to finish their degree. It is strongly recommended that students complete all of their required General Chemistry courses at a single institution. Additional Information for Transfer Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows. For additional information on getting started in this major, visit the Molecular, Cell and Developmental Biology Transfer Preparation guide . Visit the Molecular, Cell and Developmental Biology (MCD) website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Molecular, Cell, and Developmental Biology B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below, each with a grade of C (2.0) or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall.


NOTE: BIOL 20L is not required for students who complete the articulated equivalents of BIOL 20A and BIOE 20B at California community colleges. Refer to "Required Major Preparation Courses," Group #2, Section A below for courses approved to satisfy these requirements.


Winter Applicants Screening Policy

Students applying for winter quarter admission are additionally required to complete the articulated equivalent of CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry. Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for courses approved to articulate to this requirement.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required screening courses, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, especially CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry and CHEM 8L – Organic Chemistry Laboratory. These courses serve as prerequisites for upper-division requirements, and students who do not complete them prior to transfer will likely need additional quarters to finish their degree.


It is strongly recommended that students complete all of their required General Chemistry courses at a single institution.



Additional Information for Transfer

Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows.



For additional information on getting started in this major, visit the Molecular, Cell and Developmental Biology Transfer Preparation guide.


Visit the Molecular, Cell and Developmental Biology (MCD) website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Molecular, Cell, and Developmental Biology B.S. Program Statement
